name: Dave McKenna
abstract: Dave McKenna (May 30, 1930 – October 18, 2008) was an American jazz pianist known primarily as a solo pianist and for his "three-handed" swing style. He was a significant figure in the evolution of jazz piano.

interviews.title: Dave McKenna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Daryl Sherman
about_quotes.evidence: Dave McKenna, my own hometown hero who I’d never met... I was very close with his family... Dave just by his nature and knowing my dad actually asked me if I wanted to sit in... Dave introduced me — all the musicians in town came to hear him... I adored Dave, that we had so much, you know, family in
about_quotes.doc_title: Daryl Sherman interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 11, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: George Masso
about_quotes.evidence: It’s Dick Johnson, Lou Columbo — are you familiar with that name? Dave McKenna, Marshall Wood, Artie Cabral, a drummer from Providence, and myself. It’s a sextet.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Masso interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 10, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Jake Hanna
about_quotes.evidence: to work with McKenna, you know, beautiful. Then we added Bob Wilber and Cutty Cutshall and we went out and we always had that good quartet
about_quotes.doc_title: Jake Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Temperley
about_quotes.evidence: I liked the one I did with Dave McKenna... The duet, yeah. I love playing with Dave McKenna.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Temperley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Allen
about_quotes.evidence: I’m lucky enough to get to work with Dave McKenna quite a bit
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 13, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: Like we recorded Jimmie Rowles, and we recorded Dave McKenna.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
